question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
answers: nan

question: Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers: All studies related to the treatment of DMC published in English or French until the search date were included in the systematic review. Only the studies about DMC treatment were selected.

question: Were eligibility criteria unambiguous?
answers:
All studies related to the treatment of DMC published in English or French until the search date were included in the systematic review. Only the studies about DMC treatment were selected. Cure was
defined as complete healing of the cyst with no recurrence
during the reported follow-up period. Patients lost to follow-up
were excluded from this study. All included articles clearly
describe the treatment modality and the number of cured
lesions. Exclusion criteria were (i) case-series with
five lesions or less and (ii) studies using concomitant
treatments.

question: Were all restrictions in eligibility crieria based on study characteristics appropriate (e.g. date, sample size, study quality, outcomes measured)? If yes, indicate which study characteristic was an inclusion/exclusion criteria
answers: Patients lost to follow-up were excluded from this study. All included articles clearly describe the treatment modality and the number of cured lesions. Exclusion criteria were (i) case-series with five lesions or less and (ii) studies using concomitant treatments.

question: Did the search include an appropriate range of databases/ electronic sources for published and unpublished reports?
answers: we conducted a systematic review of published articles in MEDLINE, EMBASE, and Cochrane databases

question: Were methods additional to database searching used to identify relevant reports?
answers: nan

question: Were the terms and structure of the search strategy likely to retrieve as many eligible studies as possible?
answers:
The search was done with no restrictions using
specific terms for DMC ("ganglionic cyst" or "ganglion cyst" or
"myxoid cyst" or "mucous cyst" or "synovial cyst") combined
with keywords to narrow the search to cysts located on
fingers or toes ("digit" or "digits" or "digital" or "thumb" or
"index" or "finger" or "toe" or "toes").

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: The search was done with no restrictions

question: Were all pre-defined analyses reported or departures explained?
answers: nan

question: Was the synthesis appropriate given the nature and similarity in the research questions, study designs and outcomes across included studies?
answers: Cure rates between the different treatment modalities were compared using a chi-square test. A significance level of 0.05 was chosen.

question: Was between-study variation (heterogeneity) minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers: Cure rates between the different treatment modalities were compared using a chi-square test. A significance level of 0.05 was chosen.

question: Were the findings robust, e.g. as demonstrated through funnel plot or sensitivity analyses?
answers: Cure rates between the different treatment modalities were compared using a chi-square test. A significance level of 0.05 was chosen.

Study type (GRADE)a

Other limitations were inter-practitioner disparities and publication bias as only published articles were evaluated.

question: Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers:
There are several limitations to this review. Thirty-four of the included studies were retrospective and six were prospective trials.
We did not find any randomized controlled trials comparing different treatment methods, which prevented us from performing a meta-analysis. Other limitations were inter-practitioner disparities and publication bias as only published articles were evaluated.
